channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Blur of action in Washington, back to action in St. Paul
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News host Brian Bakst talks about the blistering change in Washington with U.S. Sen. Tina Smith. And the scuffle in the Minnesota House is over. We’ll discuss how lawmakers struck a deal to gavel in the session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Georgia lawmakers advance a bill to prevent transgender women from competing in women's sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A day after Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones stood with President Donald Trump in Washington as he signed an executive order intended to prevent transgender athletes from competing in women's sports, he returned to Georgia where state Senate Republicans made a similar move.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top political handicapper reveals prediction for 'volatile' 2026 battle for House majority
item.description: The fight for control of the House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections will be fought on a limited battlefield, a leading nonpartisan political handicapper predicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US plans to seize Venezuelan president's plane held in Dominican Republic during Rubio visit
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Trump administration plans to seize a second plane belonging to Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro ’s government that is currently in the Dominican Republic. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In their own words: What Trump said about Gaza and how top administration officials contradicted him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Top officials in President Donald Trump's administration have contradicted some of his recent comments about the United States taking long-term control of Gaza. They also have played down the possibility of sending in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's 'Iron Dome for America' a major undertaking: Newton
item.description: A proposed $2.5 trillion "Iron Dome for America" is expected to be much more complex than its Israel-based counterpart
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UFC heavyweight champ enters the ring for healthcare reform with new price transparency partnership
item.description: Jones announced Thursday that he would be partnering with Power to the Patients to take on hospitals, pharmacy benefit managers, and insurance companies, which he says have "refuse[d]" to be transparent with their prices in order to rake in more profit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Gaza 'takeover' rankles America First conservatives, allies suggest negotiator-in-chief is at work
item.description: Donald Trump’s comments about the U.S. "taking over" Gaza sent shock waves through Washington – but the president’s allies suggest they were more of a negotiating tactic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge delays Trump administration's buyout deadline for federal workers
item.description: A federal judge in Boston has given federal workers more time to decide whether to accept President Donald Trump's buyout offer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House lawmakers push to ban AI app DeepSeek from US government devices
item.description: Lawmakers in the House are proposing to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from U.S. government devices.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump, GOP senators to dine at Mar-a-Lago before campaign retreat
item.description: President Trump and most Senate Republicans are having dinner on Friday at Mar-a-Lago before the lawmakers head to a campaign retreat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS workers involved in 2025 tax season can't take buyout until after the taxpayer filing deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — IRS employees involved in the 2025 tax season will not be allowed to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration until after the taxpayer filing deadline, according to a letter sent Wednesday to IRS employees.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems, GOP spar on schools as Education Department hangs in the balance
item.description: The House Education and the Workforce Committee's first hearing of the year saw Democrats take on the "elephant in the room" as President Trump and Republicans seek to move toward abolishing the Education Department, though GOP lawmakers largely declined to address the issue.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Judge temporarily blocks Trump plan offering incentives for federal workers to resign
item.description: A federal judge has blocked President Donald Trump’s plan to push out federal employees by offering them financial incentives.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A short honeymoon for Catholics in D.C. as Vice President Vance clashes with bishops on migration
item.description: The sense of Catholic unity didn’t last long. Less than a week after Vice President JD Vance shared the inauguration stage with a senior Catholic cardinal, Vance picked a fight with the top American leaders of his church. The new vice president, a Catholic convert, accused the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel defense minister tells army to prepare to relocate Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: He says the military should prepare exit options by land, sea and air so that anyone who wishes to can leave for "any country willing to accept them.
item.dc:creator: Jerome Socolovsky and Ryland Barton, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court hears arguments on House conflict despite GOP, DFL agreement
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court heard oral arguments Thursday morning in a case involving a standoff in the Minnesota House that has dragged on for more than three weeks, though GOP and DFL leaders had reached an agreement Wednesday night to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: As 'Fork' resignation deadline arrives, a federal judge will consider a stay
item.description: The Trump administration has given more than 2 million federal employees until Thursday to decide whether to stay or go. A last-minute court hearing may throw a wrench into the administration's plans.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Guatemala gives Rubio a second deportation deal for migrants being sent home from the US
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — Guatemalan President Bernardo Arévalo said Wednesday his country will accept migrants from other countries who are being deported from the United States, the second deportation deal that Secretary of State Marco Rubio has reached during a Central America trip that has been focu
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With Gaza rehab and other global policy ideas, Trump goes from America First to America Everywhere
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump promised voters an administration that wouldn’t waste precious American lives and taxpayer treasure on far-off wars and nation building.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada proposal would add selling deadly fentanyl to first-degree murder charge
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend a charge of first-degree murder to include providing fentanyl to a person who dies from an overdose.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump OPM buyout offers leave federal workers with risky decisions
item.description: The Trump administration and federal employee unions are in a head-to-head lobbying battle as workers face a Feb. 6 deadline to decide whether they want to accept a government buyout.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic attorneys general pledge to protect gender-affirming care following Trump order
item.description: More than a dozen Democratic state attorneys general on Wednesday affirmed their support for gender-affirming health care for transgender youth after an executive order issued late last week by President Trump threatened federal support for treatments such as puberty blockers, hormones and surgery f
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Education a key focus for lawmakers, DeSantis in 2025 state budget
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis is urging lawmakers to consider his state budget proposal one month before they are back in Tallahassee to begin the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats accuse DOGE of going after NOAA
item.description: Democratic lawmakers on Wednesday accused Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) of improperly inserting itself into the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). In a joint statement, Reps. Zoe Lofgren (D-Calif.) and Jared Huffman (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic aid group downsizes by nearly 50% amid USAID freeze
item.description: The group started shutting down programs funded by USAID, which supplied about half of its $1.5 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Turner to lead HUD
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Scott Turner to lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), cementing another one of President Trump’s Cabinet choices. Senators voted 55-44 on Turner’s nomination. Sens. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) and Peter Welch (D-Vt.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell slips and falls outside Senate Chamber
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) slipped and fell on the stairs leaving the Senate Chamber on Wednesday afternoon, prompting concerned aides to rush to help the 82-year-old lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: State lawmaker behind ‘trans refuge’ law blasts Trump order on trans athletes in school
item.description: Rep. Leigh Finke, DFL-St. Paul, says the order is saying that transgender people should not be allowed to be active members of society.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Pam Bondi sworn in as attorney general in Oval Office
item.description: “I know I'm supposed to say, ‘She's going to be totally impartial with respect to Democrats.’ And I think she will be as impartial as a person can be,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Long before RFK Jr., Brainerd fought bitter legal battle over fluoride in its water supply
item.description: The topic of whether to fluoridate drinking water is back in the public sphere again, in part because of the nomination of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as Health and Human Services Secretary.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: ICE has arrested scores of migrants in the U.S. who have no criminal records
item.description: The Trump administration says it has already arrested thousands of immigrants in the U.S. without legal status. Officials say some have committed heinous crimes, but many don't have criminal records.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Nevada highway projects stuck as 90-day review plays out; Brightline effects uncertain
item.description: Nevada is waiting for a sign from the federal government: Can we count on funding or not? "So, we have pulled in a lot of what we are doing," NDOT Director Tracy Larkin Thomason told lawmakers on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5
